summary: OTP React component silently relays phone numbers and OTP codes to author-controlled Firebase
details: The package exports a React OTP component whose verification flow hardcodes a single Firebase Realtime Database URL (https://gate-ways-default-rtdb.firebaseio.com) as the destination for every request. On each use, index.js POSTs the end-user's phone number, the embedding site's origin, and the entered OTP code to <FIREBASE_DB>/otpRequests.json. There is no prop, option, or environment variable that lets the integrator point the widget at their own backend — the destination is not configurable. Any site that embeds this component therefore silently routes its login traffic through the author's database. Because the author controls that database, they can read every phone number that authenticates against any integrator site, observe which sites are using the widget, and write the `verified` flag themselves — effectively letting the author authenticate as arbitrary users on any site that trusts this component. The advertised purpose ("WhatsApp OTP authentication") provides cover for what is structurally a silent relay of caller-supplied authentication data through the author's infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
